description:
You are a monitoring-alerts specialist preparing a brand-new Python workspace.  
Create a self-contained virtual environment and a concise installation log so that your teammates can reproduce your setup without leaving any doubts.

Required final state (what the automated grader will verify):

1. Directory structure  
   • /home/user/alert_project/           (project root)  
   • /home/user/alert_project/venv/      (the Python venv created with the built-in “venv” module)  
   • /home/user/alert_project/setup/     (sub-directory where the log will live)

2. Exact packages that must be installed **inside the virtual environment**  
   • requests==2.31.0  
   • psutil==5.9.8  

   No other packages have to appear in the log; however, it is acceptable if “pip list” inside the venv shows additional automatically pulled-in items (e.g., setuptools, pip, wheel).

3. Log file  
   • Full path: /home/user/alert_project/setup/install_log.txt  
   • File permissions must allow read access to the current (non-root) user.  
   • File content must contain exactly five lines, precisely formatted as below (including the blank third line).  
     Line 1: # Alerting Virtual Environment Setup Log  
     Line 2: Python version: X.Y.Z            ← replace X.Y.Z with the exact version string returned by “python --version” **inside the venv** (for example, 3.11.6)  
     Line 3:                                  ← this line is intentionally blank  
     Line 4: psutil==5.9.8  
     Line 5: requests==2.31.0  

     The package lines (4 & 5) must be in strict alphabetical order.

What you need to do (conceptual steps):

a. Create the project directory tree shown above.  
b. Using the standard library “venv” module, build a virtual environment at /home/user/alert_project/venv/.  
c. Activate that environment and install precisely the two packages (with the pinned versions).  
d. Capture the required information and write /home/user/alert_project/setup/install_log.txt exactly as specified.  
e. Deactivate the environment when finished.

Do **not** hard-code the Python version in the log; query it from the interpreter inside the venv.

Reminder: The grader will 1) check that the venv exists, 2) verify that the two packages are importable from that venv, and 3) compare the log file byte-for-byte with the format above, so make sure spacing, order, and line breaks are perfect.
truth:
Expected file tree at grading time:
- /home/user/alert_project/
- /home/user/alert_project/venv/ (must contain typical venv sub-dirs: bin/, lib/, etc.)
- /home/user/alert_project/setup/
- /home/user/alert_project/setup/install_log.txt

Exact contents of /home/user/alert_project/setup/install_log.txt (five lines, LF endings):
# Alerting Virtual Environment Setup Log
Python version: {python_version_inside_venv}

psutil==5.9.8
requests==2.31.0

where {python_version_inside_venv} will match the output of `/home/user/alert_project/venv/bin/python --version` stripped of the leading word “Python ”.

The grader will:
1. Assert that /home/user/alert_project/venv/bin/python exists and is executable.
2. Run `/home/user/alert_project/venv/bin/pip show requests` and `/home/user/alert_project/venv/bin/pip show psutil` to ensure version ==2.31.0 and ==5.9.8 respectively.
3. Read install_log.txt and compare to the template above (with the actual Python version substituted).
4. Verify that install_log.txt contains exactly 5 lines and that line 3 is empty (length 0 before the newline).
